scenario_title: add_rr followed by delete_rr for the same owner/type in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified ordering/cancellation behavior in a single RFC 2136 UPDATE: the same A record is added and then deleted before the message ends. If a server simulates the operations sequentially, the final zone state is empty for that name; if it coalesces the change set, it may also end up empty but with a different internal/response path; if it treats the sequence as contradictory, it may return an error such as NXRRSET. A follow-up query for the touched name makes the final state observable.

group_1.behavior: Applies UPDATE operations sequentially or as a net no-op and returns success; the RR is absent afterward.
group_1.post_state: NXDOMAIN or NODATA for y.campus.edu. A

group_2.behavior: Rejects or otherwise treats the intra-message add-then-delete of the same RR as contradictory, producing a different UPDATE result and/or leaving different state.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 defines add_rr and delete_rr primitives, but does not specify whether the UPDATE section is processed strictly in message order, batched as a set of net modifications, or rejected when the same RR is both added and removed within one packet. That leaves both the reply code and the resulting zone state underspecified.
